speaker: CAPULET
text: Things have fallen out, sir, so unluckily

speaker: CAPULET
text: That we have had no time to move our daughter.

speaker: CAPULET
text: Look you, she loved her kinsman Tybalt dearly,

speaker: CAPULET
text: And so did I. Well, we were born to die.

speaker: CAPULET
text: ’Tis very late. She’ll not come down tonight.

speaker: CAPULET
text: I promise you, but for your company,

speaker: CAPULET
text: I would have been abed an hour ago.

speaker: PARIS
text: These times of woe afford no times to woo.—

speaker: PARIS
text: Madam, good night. Commend me to your daughter.

speaker:
LADY
               
              CAPULET
text: I will, and know her mind early tomorrow.

speaker:
LADY
               
              CAPULET
text: Tonight she’s mewed up to her heaviness.

speaker: CAPULET
text: Sir Paris, I will make a desperate tender

speaker: CAPULET
text: Of my child’s love. I think she will be ruled

speaker: CAPULET
text: In all respects by me. Nay, more, I doubt it not.—

speaker: CAPULET
text: Wife, go you to her ere you go to bed.

speaker: CAPULET
text: Acquaint her here of my son Paris’ love,

speaker: CAPULET
text: And bid her—mark you me?—on Wednesday next—

speaker: CAPULET
text: But soft, what day is this?

speaker: PARIS
text: Monday, my lord.

speaker: CAPULET
text: Monday, ha ha! Well, Wednesday is too soon.

speaker: CAPULET
text: O’ Thursday let it be.—O’ Thursday, tell her,

speaker: CAPULET
text: She shall be married to this noble earl.—

speaker: CAPULET
text: Will you be ready? Do you like this haste?

speaker: CAPULET
text: We’ll keep no great ado: a friend or two.

speaker: CAPULET
text: For hark you, Tybalt being slain so late,

speaker: CAPULET
text: It may be thought we held him carelessly,

speaker: CAPULET
text: Being our kinsman, if we revel much.

speaker: CAPULET
text: Therefore we’ll have some half a dozen friends,

speaker: CAPULET
text: And there an end. But what say you to Thursday?

speaker: PARIS
text: My lord, I would that Thursday were tomorrow.

speaker: CAPULET
text: Well, get you gone. O’ Thursday be it, then.

speaker: CAPULET
text:
To
                 
                Lady
                 
                Capulet
                .
                 
              Go you to Juliet ere you go to bed.

speaker: CAPULET
text: Prepare her, wife, against this wedding day.—

speaker: CAPULET
text: Farewell, my lord.—Light to my chamber, ho!—

speaker: CAPULET
text: Afore me, it is so very late that we

speaker: CAPULET
text: May call it early by and by.—Good night.
